h2: My Plan
h3: Protecting our green spaces from inappropriate housing developments and safeguarding our precious environment.
h3: Improving our NHS, making appointments with GPs and dentists easier, and cutting waiting times at hospitals.
h3: Investing in schools and ensuring funding for a first-class education for all our children.
h3: Working with Kent Police to increase uniformed presence and reduce crime and anti-social behaviour in our communities.
h3: Supporting local businesses to help them flourish, and create apprenticeships and jobs for local people.
h3: Battling for better road, rail and bus transport.

pages.meta_description: Political Helen became actively involved in politics in 2006 campaigning with the Reigate and Banstead Conservatives in local elections.

pages.text_excerpt: About Helen Grant | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey About Helen Grant Tweet Political Helen became actively involved in politics in 2006 campaigning with the Reigate and Banstead Conservatives in local elections. After being a finalist at her first two candidate selections, she was chosen from almost 100 applicants as the Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for Maidstone & The Weald in January 2008. She campaigned there for the next two years prior to being elected in the May 2010 general election. She is the first Anglo-African female Conservative MP and Minister. She has since been returned as the MP for Maidstone & The Weald in the General Elections of May 2015, June 2017 and December 2019 with ever growing local support. After serving on the House of Commons Justice Select Committee she was promoted by David Cameron as Minister for Justice 2012-13 and Minister for Women and Equalities 2012-2014. During that time, Helen was one of the three ministers responsible for taking the historic ‘Marriage (Same Sex Couples) Bill’ through the House of Commons. As Minister for Sport and Tourism from 2013-2015 she notably championed the growth of women and girls’ participation in sport in the UK. She achieved this through, among other things, setting up a Women in Sport advisory board, launching the #ThisGirlCan campaign and holding the first DCMS Women’s Sport Conference in October 2014. https://www.womeninsport.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/05/Womens-Sport-Conference-Interactive-PDF.pdf She also drove forward a similar agenda for people from diverse and under-represented communities. She stepped down from Government after the 2015 general Election and then served as a Trustee for the Human Trafficking Foundation, a Trustee of the Social Mobility Foundation, and an elected member of the International Development Select Committee. During a meeting with Nigerian Vice President Osinbajo in London in 2016, Helen was asked to use her experience as a lawyer and as a politician to help strengthen the Rule of Law in Nigeria. She proceeded to instigate an initiative to help train judges and staff in the lower tier courts, working alongside the Judicial College in London and State Governors and Attorney Generals in a growing number of Nigerian states. These now include the Federal Capital Territory, Niger, Sokoto, Imo, Kaduna, Bauchi and Plateau and it is an ongoing success story. In October 2017 she was appointed Chair of the Government’s Apprenticeships Diversity Champions Network, bringing businesses together with people of diverse and under-represented backgrounds using the transformational opportunities of apprenticeship programmes. Under Prime Minister Theresa May, Helen served as the Conservative Party’s Vice Chair for Communities for 2 years from 2018 to 2020. During this period she travelled extensively and focussed upon issues concerning equality, inclusivity, social cohesion, racism, and discrimination, engaging deeply with the UK’s many ethnic groups and communities. In October 2020 Helen was appointed by Prime Minister Boris Johnson as the UK Trade Envoy to Nigeria, the largest market in the African Continent with a population of over 200 million people. She is supporting the drive for economic growth by building on the UK’s existing relations with Nigeria, maximising bilateral trade and generating real and long term benefits for the UK. Helen has a special relationship with Nigeria, where her father was born and raised, and where she has had strong personal connections for many years. https://www.gov.uk/government/groups/trade-envoys In January 2021 she was also appointed as the Prime Minister's Special Envoy for Girls Education, leading the UK’s efforts internationally to ensure all girls get 12 years of quality education. One of her goals is to drive a global campa

pages.text_excerpt: Advice Surgeries | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Advice Surgeries Tweet If you would like to visit me in my constituency office, arrange a virtual meeting on Zoom or talk on the phone, then please firstly send your personal details including your full postal address along with a short summary of the matter you wish to discuss to me via any of the contact methods listed here (email or postal). I host regular surgeries across different Maidstone and Malling wards. Keep informed of my upcoming community outreach surgeries by looking on my social media pages , or email for more information. My upcoming dates are: Thursday 29th January Please note that, due to Parliamentary protocol, I am only able to arrange surgery appointments for people who live within the Maidstone and Malling Parliamentary Constituency. Check 'My Map' on the homepage to see if you live within the boundaries, or use the 'Am I your MP?' tool and use your postcode to find out. Experience has shown that most issues can be actioned more quickly by e-mail, letter or telephone, so please do feel free to seek preliminary advice from my team as to how your case may be best resolved. Surgeries are confidential. Video and phone surgeries are also attended by a trained Caseworker from my personal staff who then deals with the follow-up work under my supervision. pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Contact Leave this field blank Constituency Office First Floor, 109 Week Street, Maidstone, Kent , ME14 1RB 01622 669623 [email protected] Helen on Twitter Helen on Instagram Helen's team are available by phone Monday-Friday, 9am-5pm except for public holidays and the extended Christmas and New Year period. You can get in touch by phone or email, or alternatively you can send a message via the webform below. Please note when contacting Helen about potential casework, it is essential that constituents provide their full residential postal address (including postcode) and a contact telephone number. Constituency Office Helen's constituency office is located at the north end of Week Street, above 'Pets Corner.' Car Parking is available at a range of public car parks nearby. These include Maidstone East Railway Station and Brewer Street car parks. Media enquiries For Media enquiries, contact Helen's office via [email protected] Press Releases are uploaded to the 'News' section of this website. If you are a local or national journalist and would like to be added to Helen's press distribution list, then please e-mail your request. Delivering a few leaflets locally now and again Helping with office and admin tasks Making calls to voters and volunteers Joining our canvassing team to knock on doors Potentially standing to be a local Conservative Councillor Becoming a Digital Ambassador By agreeing to volunteer for us, you also agree to us contacting you about volunteering. pages.meta_description: There will be few people or households across the entire country that will be unaffected by the rising costs of living - firstly in the wake of the pandemic, and now due to the escalating global effects of Russia's war against Ukraine.

h2: Cost of Living Help Hub
h3: Helen Grant Conservative Candidate for Maidstone and Malling
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: Helen Grant MP's cost-of-living 'Help Hub' | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Helen Grant MP's cost-of-living 'Help Hub' Tweet There will be few people or households across the entire country that will be unaffected by the rising costs of living - firstly in the wake of the pandemic, and now due to the escalating global effects of Russia's war against Ukraine. My team and I are helping constituents every day with their individual challenges and we will continue to do so throughout these exceptionally difficult times. To help signpost people to specific points of contact we have also created this online 'cost of living support hub'. From welfare benefits, like universal credit, to where to get help with gas and electricity bills, the hub also includes information on help with council tax, housing costs, how to access food banks and Government support schemes as well as how to contact voluntary organisations working across the area. It also includes certain specialist financial advisory organisations who have the regulatory authority to provide individual financial or legal advice. Cost of Living Help Hub Citizens Advice Bureaux Household Bills, Utilities, Winter Fuel & Council Tax Income Support, Universal Credit and benefits Help with Childcare Costs Food Banks and Pantries Housing and Rental Cost Help Healthcare and Disability Support Help with Travel Costs Debt Advice and Support Special Advice for Older People Helen Grant Conservative Candidate for Maidstone and Malling Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy About Helen Grant Promoted by Thomas Mobbs on behalf of Helen Grant MP and Maidstone and Malling Conservative Association, both of Suite 2 Paddock Wood Business Centre, Commercial Road, Paddock Wood, TN12 6EN Copyright 2026 Helen Grant. pages.text_excerpt: Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Tweet Dear constituent, When you or someone in your family is ill, you want to be able to see your local GP and get the help you need. An excellent GP practice does much more than write prescriptions and make referrals – it looks after you physically and mentally, helps you manage if you have a long-term condition and cares for the community. Unfortunately, people often tell me that it’s difficult to get an appointment. That’s why I’m getting in touch to find out more about your experiences at your local surgery. Please fill out this survey and return it to me so I can help you get better healthcare. Best wishes, Helen Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Current Your details GP Survey Leave this field blank First name Last name Email Postcode Address ID Address Parliamentary constituency Admin county Admin district CED Admin ward Parish I would like to be kept up-to-date by the Conservatives on future issues that matter to me: Yes There will be an easy way to opt out of future messages. pages.text_excerpt: Protecting our green spaces from inappropriate housing developments and safeguarding our precious environment. | Helen Grant Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Helen News Advice Surgeries My Plan In Parliament Contact Cost of Living Help Hub Apprenticeship Portal Maidstone & Malling GP Surgery Survey Protecting our green spaces from inappropriate housing developments and safeguarding our precious environment. Tweet This has been a pledge since I was first elected as MP in 2010, starting with the Save Fant Farm campaign. We now face challenges with the huge Bradbourne and Broadwater Farm developments in the Mallings, and the threat to Bunyards Farm in Allington News Helen Grant MP Writes to MBC Planning Committee to Oppose Bunyards Planning Application Thursday, 23 April, 2026 Ahead of tonight's MBC planning committee, I have sent the following statement to the committee members, outlining my strong opposition to the Bunyards planning application: Helen Grant MP Details Problems with Over-Development in the County Wednesday, 8 April, 2026 Throughout my time as a Member of Parliament here in the Garden of England, overdevelopment has been one of the most persistent concerns raised by constituents.I have consistently worked to protect our precious green spaces from inappropriate development, from fighting to save Fant Farm in Maidstone Helen Grant MP writes to Tonbridge and Malling Borough Council to oppose 1,300-home Bradbourne development in Aylesford Thursday, 5 March, 2026 Helen Grant MP has written to Tonbridge and Malling Borough Councillors urging them to reject an application to build a new community of 1,300 homes to be known as Bradbourne on land east of Kiln Barn Road and west of Hermitage Lane in Aylesford. Helen Grant Condemns No-Show of South East Water CEO David Hinton at Tunbridge Wells Borough Council Scrutiny Committee Friday, 23 January, 2026 Amidst the turmoil and failure caused by South East Water, South East Water CEO David Hinton shamefully refused to attend the Tunbridge Wells Borough Council Scrutiny Committee, on the very same day that invitations were sent by SEW invited MPs to a webinar to discuss bill increases. Maidstone and Malling MP Helen Grant complains of too many road closures - and residents agree Saturday, 17 January, 2026 Motorists must be able to go about their daily lives without sitting in unnecessary and prolonged traffic. Please read more here: Calls for water boss to quit amid latest supply crisis Tuesday, 13 January, 2026 Once again, South East Water has failed, and once again there has been a shocking lack of leadership.This is no isolated incident, it is now a familiar and unacceptable pattern across Kent and Sussex.It’s been like the nightmare before Christmas we experienced for 12 days in 2022. Statement on Ivy Farm Planning Application Monday, 13 January, 2025 Ahead of Wednesday's TMBC planning committee, please see my below statement on the Ivy Farm planning application:Throughout my time as a Member of Parliament, overdevelopment has been a persistent issue raised by constituents, and I have fought hard to protect our precious green spaces from inapprop Helen Grant MP leads ‘time sensitive’ charge against 1300 houses between Hermitage Lane and Ditton Friday, 19 April, 2024 April 2024, Maidstone/Malling, Kent: The fight against overdevelopment in the Hermitage Lane area stepped up a gear after a packed public meeting and official opposition from Helen Grant MP last week. April Newsletter Friday, 5 April, 2024 My team and I have been as busy as ever throughout March with a wide array of activities in Parliament, Maidstone, and the Weald. You can read my full April newsletter here. Statement on Bradbourne Planning Application Thursday, 28 March, 2024 A statement regarding the new ‘Bradbourne’ planning application for up to 1300 houses between Hermitage Lane and Kiln Barn Road, Ditton: These housing development proposals will impose impossible new pressures on local in
